title: EasyJet routes could produce 'significant growth'
content:
The prospect of EasyJet offering new routes to Guernsey could "produce significant growth", a deputy has said.

The States of Guernsey confirmed it has beentalking to EasyJet about potential new routes to the islandon Thursday. The BBC understands the destinations could include Liverpool and Luton airports.

The Guernsey Aviation Action Group (GAAG), a campaign group for improved affordable air links, said it welcomed the idea of new routes as it opened doors for the tourism sector.

Deputy Simon Vermeulen, a member of the Committee for Home Affairs, said it would be a positive development for the island.

He said: "You've got to remember we've lost Flybe and we've lost Blue Islands, so we do need to pick up new carriers for other destinations and we've landed British Airways which is considerable.

"I think EasyJet to Luton and Liverpool or any other destinations with their database could produce significant growth for the island and would be great for islanders too."

Joe Mooney, from GAAG, said: "Luton opens up Europe, but also the important thing for Liverpool - that gives access to 1.5 million people to come to Guernsey.

"Liverpool is one of the most visited cities in the UK for international tourists so very good hub to connect for Guernsey."
